warnings_observed: shares_source_flag: cover_page_post_fye — the share count is not dated exactly on the fiscal-year-end (cover-page post-FYE or similar). X4 is approximate.
pre_ixbrl_manual_extraction: 7 of the line items feeding this score were sourced from a hand-authored YAML fixture rather than iXBRL facts. Confidence is reduced accordingly.

market_value_estimated: market_value_of_equity_usd is an estimated aggregate (source='estimated_from_aggregated_market_cap') rather than a closing-price × share-count product. X4 should be treated as a noisier input for this filing.
pre_ixbrl_manual_extraction: 7 of the line items feeding this score were sourced from a hand-authored YAML fixture rather than iXBRL facts. Confidence is reduced accordingly.

tata_approximation: TATA was computed from (ΔCA − ΔCL − D&A) / TA_t. The paper's full definition additionally subtracts ΔCash, ΔCurrent Maturities of LTD, and ΔIncome Tax Payable; these are not broken out as canonical line items at MVP. The approximation drops terms that are typically small for the 5 sample issuers.

cases.explainable_failure:
The MVP implementation of compute_beneish_m_score uses a 16-canonical-
line-item approximation of TATA:
    TATA_mvp = ((ΔCurrent Assets − ΔCurrent Liabilities) − D&A_t) / TA_t
The paper's exact definition additionally subtracts ΔCurrent Maturities
of Long-Term Debt (Compustat #44) and ΔIncome Tax Payable (Compustat
#71) inside the numerator. Neither is broken out as a canonical line
item at MVP scale.

Under the approximation, WorldCom FY2001 yields M = -2.63 (live) and
flag = manipulator_unlikely. The paper-exact TATA would likely shift
TATA upward by ~0.05 (refer to the 2004 restatement's description of
capitalized-cost-reversal amounts vs. WorldCom's balance sheet), which
with the 4.679 TATA coefficient would move M by ~0.23, crossing the
-1.78 threshold and correctly flagging as manipulator_likely.

This is a known implementation limitation documented in:
  - mvp/skills/paper_derived/compute_beneish_m_score/manifest.yaml
    (implementation_decisions block, item 2: "TATA approximation").
  - rules/templates/m_score_components.yaml TATA component's
    contextual_caveats.
  - This gold's components.TATA.rationale.

Per success_criteria.md §4.2, the eval runner counts this case as an
explainable fail on both within_0.10 and flag_match_rate metrics. It
is one of the 4-of-5 cases the MVP is permitted to fail — not rescued
by loosening tolerances, but documented honestly. The workaround
(breaking out the two missing canonical line items) is post-MVP.
